title: rle — run-length encode with singleton rule
prompt: Write a Python function with this exact signature:
It run-length-encodes the string: replace each maximal run of one repeated
character with that character followed by the run length in decimal, EXCEPT a
run of length 1, which is written as just the character (no digit).
The encoding is case-sensitive ('a' and 'A' are different characters).
The empty string encodes to the empty string.
Respond with a SINGLE Python code block (```python ... ```) defining rle and
nothing else. Include no explanation outside the code block.
difficulty_rationale: A single linear pass with two twists that hidden asserts probe: multi-digit counts ('a10', not 'aaaaaaaaaa' or 'a1...') and case sensitivity ('wwwWWW' -> 'w3W3'). The singleton-no-digit rule is the main correctness trap. Expected medium+ solid; low may emit counts for singletons.
